description: Unify cloud and local execution behind the same primary commands (run/auto/review --cloud), provide minimal cloud support surface (setup/doctor/list/status/logs/cancel/pull/auth), remove legacy command variants, enforce non-secret project config, and provide predictable diagnostics and follow-up controls.

userStories.title: Define .hal/cloud.yaml profile schema
userStories.description: As a product engineer, I want a clear non-secret cloud config schema so that setup is predictable and safe.
acceptanceCriteria: .hal/cloud.yaml schema supports default profile name and per-profile defaults (mode/endpoints/repo/base/engine/auth profile/scope/wait/pull policy)
Schema validation errors include field path, failed rule, and remediation message for each invalid or missing required value
Schema explicitly excludes secret-bearing fields (tokens/passwords/DSNs with secrets)
Loading and validation are covered by automated tests for valid and invalid files
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Persist workflow kind in cloud run records
userStories.description: As the cloud runtime, I want each run to persist run|auto|review kind so that workers execute the correct workflow command.
acceptanceCriteria: Cloud run persistence includes a required workflowKind field with allowed values run, auto, review
Run submission from each workflow command stores the correct workflowKind
Worker/job loading fails fast with a clear error if workflowKind is missing or invalid
Automated tests verify serialization/deserialization and validation behavior
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Define artifact-group metadata for pull behavior
userStories.description: As a user, I want deterministic artifact groups so that hal cloud pull can fetch state, reports, or all reliably.
acceptanceCriteria: Cloud run artifacts are categorized into state and reports groups in persisted metadata
--artifacts state|reports|all maps to deterministic file selection
Auto/review runs include report artifacts; all workflows include continuation state artifacts
Automated tests verify artifact selection for each workflow kind
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Remove legacy cloud command aliases after unified rollout
userStories.description: As a user, I want obsolete cloud command aliases removed after replacements exist so help output stays simple and unambiguous.
acceptanceCriteria: After unified replacements are available, hal cloud run and hal cloud submit are removed
hal cloud runs namespace plus hal cloud smoke and hal cloud env are removed
hal cloud --help does not list removed commands
Invoking each removed command returns unknown-command behavior with non-zero exit status
Snapshot tests verify command-tree and unknown-command behavior for removed aliases
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement shared cloud precedence resolver
userStories.description: As a developer, I want one resolver for cloud defaults so precedence behavior is consistent across workflows.
acceptanceCriteria: A shared resolver composes values with precedence: CLI > process env > .env (non-overriding) > .hal/cloud.yaml > inferred defaults > hard defaults
Resolver returns the same resolved runtime config shape for run, auto, and review inputs
When cloud mode is disabled, resolver outputs cloudEnabled=false and preserves existing local defaults for run/auto/review (verified by golden tests)
Unit tests cover precedence at each source tier and fallback behavior
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add hal cloud list command
userStories.description: As a user, I want to list cloud runs quickly so that I can find run IDs and statuses for follow-up actions.
acceptanceCriteria: hal cloud list returns up to 20 runs ordered by updated timestamp descending, including run ID, workflow kind, status, and updated timestamp
hal cloud list --json returns valid JSON with no mixed plain-text lines
Empty-state behavior is explicit in human mode and valid in JSON mode
Command-level tests cover success, empty list, and store error paths
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement guided hal cloud setup
userStories.description: As a new user, I want guided setup so that I can configure cloud defaults without editing files manually.
acceptanceCriteria: hal cloud setup prompts for profile defaults and writes .hal/cloud.yaml
Setup writes only non-secret values
Re-running setup can update the selected profile without deleting unrelated profiles
Setup output prints profile name, selected endpoint, and default workflow mode
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement hal cloud doctor
userStories.description: As a user, I want a diagnostic command so that I can resolve setup/auth/connectivity issues quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: hal cloud doctor validates profile/config resolution
Doctor performs explicit checks for profile resolution, endpoint reachability, and auth profile validity

Blocking failures include category-specific next-step guidance: config -> rerun hal cloud setup, auth -> link/import credentials for the active profile, connectivity -> verify endpoint and rerun hal cloud doctor
Exit code is non-zero for blocking failures and zero for pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add cloud execution path for hal run --cloud
userStories.description: As a user, I want hal run --cloud to submit and track a cloud run so that cloud feels like local run with remote execution.
acceptanceCriteria: hal run --cloud submits a run with workflowKind=run
Wait/detach behavior follows resolved defaults and explicit flags
Human output includes run ID, current status, and next-step command hints
JSON mode returns valid machine-readable submission/progress/summary payloads
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add cloud execution path for hal auto --cloud
userStories.description: As a user, I want hal auto --cloud to submit with workflowKind=auto so that auto workflows use the same cloud model.
acceptanceCriteria: hal auto --cloud submits with workflowKind=auto
Command reuses shared cloud resolution and submit/wait behavior
Completion summary references report and state artifact availability
JSON mode is valid JSON with required fields runId, workflowKind, and status
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add cloud execution path for hal review --cloud
userStories.description: As a user, I want hal review --cloud to submit with workflowKind=review so that review workflows use the same cloud model.
acceptanceCriteria: hal review --cloud submits with workflowKind=review
Command reuses shared cloud resolution and submit/wait behavior
Completion summary references report and state artifact availability
JSON mode is valid JSON with required fields runId, workflowKind, and status
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Enforce workflow-kind worker dispatch
userStories.description: As a system owner, I want workers to execute only the command matching persisted workflow kind so that jobs cannot run the wrong workflow.
acceptanceCriteria: Worker dispatch maps run -> hal run, auto -> hal auto, review -> hal review
Worker dispatch uses only workflowKind; tests assert exact argv mapping run->"hal run", auto->"hal auto", review->"hal review" and verify mode flags do not alter dispatch
Worker tests assert exact command construction by kind
Logs/events include workflow kind for observability
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement hal cloud status command
userStories.description: As a user, I want to check the status of a cloud run by ID so that I can monitor progress after submission.
acceptanceCriteria: hal cloud status <run-id> returns current run state, workflow kind, and timestamps
Command supports --json with valid JSON output
Invalid run ID returns deterministic error payload/code run_not_found including the requested run ID
Tests cover success and error paths
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement hal cloud logs command
userStories.description: As a user, I want to view logs for a cloud run so that I can debug or monitor execution.
acceptanceCriteria: hal cloud logs <run-id> returns run output logs
hal cloud logs <run-id> --follow streams logs until completion
Command supports --json with valid JSON output
Tests cover success, follow mode, and error paths
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement hal cloud cancel command
userStories.description: As a user, I want to cancel a running cloud run so that I can stop unwanted executions.
acceptanceCriteria: hal cloud cancel <run-id> cancels the specified run
Command returns confirmation of cancellation with final status
Cancelling an already-completed run returns exit code 0 with status already_terminal and the terminal status value
Command supports --json with valid JSON output
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement hal cloud pull command
userStories.description: As a user, I want to pull artifacts from a cloud run so that I can retrieve state and reports locally.
acceptanceCriteria: hal cloud pull <run-id> pulls artifacts to local .hal/ directory
hal cloud pull <run-id> --artifacts state|reports|all supports deterministic artifact selection
Pull behavior is safe for existing local files (no destructive overwrite unless explicitly forced)
Command supports --json with valid JSON output
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement hal cloud auth import validation
userStories.description: As a security-conscious user, I want auth import to validate credentials and return deterministic results.
acceptanceCriteria: hal cloud auth import validates supplied credentials with the configured backend
Invalid credentials return non-zero exit with deterministic error code invalid_credentials
Successful import persists linked credential metadata for the selected auth profile
Command tests cover import success and invalid-credential failure paths
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Standardize cloud help text and command discoverability
userStories.description: As a user, I want concise help output so that I can learn cloud commands quickly without legacy noise.
acceptanceCriteria: hal cloud --help lists only supported commands: setup, doctor, list, status, logs, cancel, pull, auth

hal run --help, hal auto --help, and hal review --help each document the same cloud flag set: --cloud, --cloud-profile, --detach, --wait, --json, --cloud-mode, --cloud-endpoint, --cloud-repo, --cloud-base, --cloud-auth-profile, --cloud-auth-scope
Removed commands do not appear in any help text
Help snapshot tests verify exact command tree and key flag docs
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Normalize hal run --cloud output contract
userStories.description: As a user and tool integrator, I want hal run --cloud outputs normalized to a deterministic contract.
acceptanceCriteria: hal run --cloud human output includes run ID after submission, terminal status at completion, and one next-step hint command
hal run --cloud --json output is valid JSON with required fields runId, workflowKind, and status
hal run --cloud prerequisite failures return deterministic error code and message fields in human and JSON modes
Command-level tests verify the hal run output contract fields for success and prerequisite-failure cases
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Enforce non-secret cloud config schema validation
userStories.description: As a security-conscious user, I want .hal/cloud.yaml schema validation to reject secret-bearing project config values.
acceptanceCriteria: .hal/cloud.yaml schema rejects secret-bearing fields and values (token/password/secret/authToken query parameters)
Schema loading fails fast before runtime resolution when secret-bearing keys are present
Validation errors include offending field path and violated rule
Unit tests cover schema rejection and error formatting for secret inputs
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Define shared cloud flag contract and conflict validation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a shared cloud-flag contract helper so command flag behavior is defined in one place.
acceptanceCriteria: A shared helper defines cloud flags: --cloud, --cloud-profile, --detach, --wait, --json, --cloud-mode, --cloud-endpoint, --cloud-repo, --cloud-base, --cloud-auth-profile, --cloud-auth-scope
The helper validates the --detach and --wait conflict and returns deterministic error code invalid_flag_combination
Unit tests verify helper-provided flag definitions and conflict validation behavior
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Redact secrets in hal run --cloud output
userStories.description: As a security-conscious user, I want hal run --cloud output to redact secrets in both human and JSON modes.
acceptanceCriteria: hal run --cloud human output replaces detected secret values with [REDACTED]
hal run --cloud --json output redacts secrets while remaining valid JSON
Security tests inject sample secrets and assert they are absent from hal run output
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add integration conformance tests for workflow cloud outputs
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want integration tests that lock workflow output contracts to prevent regressions.
acceptanceCriteria: Integration tests run hal run --cloud, hal auto --cloud, and hal review --cloud against deterministic mocked backend responses
Tests assert JSON parseability and required fields defined in the workflow output contract story
Tests assert human-mode summaries include run ID, terminal status, and next-step hint command
Integration suite includes at least one prerequisite-failure case with deterministic error fields
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Redact secrets in hal cloud auth import output
userStories.description: As a security-conscious user, I want hal cloud auth import output to redact secrets in human and JSON responses.
acceptanceCriteria: hal cloud auth import human output replaces detected secret values with [REDACTED]
hal cloud auth import --json output redacts secrets while remaining valid JSON
Security tests inject sample secrets and assert they are absent from auth import output
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement hal cloud auth status state reporting
userStories.description: As a user, I want hal cloud auth status to report deterministic credential state for a profile.
acceptanceCriteria: hal cloud auth status reports one of missing, linked, invalid, or revoked for the selected profile
hal cloud auth status --json includes profileId and status, plus lastValidatedAt when available
Command tests cover status output for missing, linked, invalid, and revoked states
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement hal cloud auth revoke transition
userStories.description: As a user, I want revoke to transition credentials into a deterministic revoked state.
acceptanceCriteria: hal cloud auth revoke transitions linked or invalid credentials to revoked for the selected profile
Revoking missing or already-revoked credentials returns deterministic non-fatal output contract
Subsequent hal cloud auth status reports revoked after successful revoke
Command tests cover revoke success and edge-state behavior
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement hal cloud auth validate state refresh
userStories.description: As a user, I want hal cloud auth validate to refresh backend credential validity deterministically.
acceptanceCriteria: hal cloud auth validate re-checks backend credentials for the selected profile and updates stored status deterministically
hal cloud auth validate --json includes profileId, status, and validatedAt fields
Command tests cover validate success and invalid-credential outcomes
Typecheck passes
